behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error (wrong artifact key on write). Required setup: run evaluate-stage command with an in-process artifact store and assert writes go to `grounding` and not `plan`. MOCK-MISS: this is beyond one pure function because command wiring + store interaction must be exercised. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; correctness does not depend on real AI output quality; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is only about artifact destination ('stores grounding into ... artifact'), not external infra or model-quality correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke evaluate-stage entrypoint with deterministic input and a real in-process artifact repository implementation (or test DB/filesystem adapter used locally). Assert: `grounding` artifact is created/updated, `plan` is untouched by this stage, and metadata/versioning are consistent.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module boundary/wiring error in read-from-one-artifact and write-to-another flow. Required setup: execute implement flow with pre-seeded `grounding` artifact and verify read path + write path together. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; behavior is routing/state use, not model-quality judgment; not a multi-step pipeline test by itself. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about artifact usage ('reads grounding... writes ... to plan'), not quality of LLM output.
behaviors.test_description: Seed artifact store with `grounding`, run implement stage with deterministic/stubbed generation, then assert implement consulted `grounding` (e.g., via trace/input payload) and emitted execution output into `plan` while leaving `grounding` intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: conditional orchestration/state-side-effect bug across issue-content intake and artifact-write layer. Required setup: run deep-dive flow with issue content containing diagnosis and verify no `plan` write side effect. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: this does not require real OS/network/subprocess if issue content is provided in-process; no AI-quality dependency is needed to detect overwrite bug; no multi-step agentic chain required. Rejected System because behavior text scopes condition to existing issue content, not real HTTP/API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue payload fixture that already includes diagnosis, execute deep-dive stage, and assert deep-dive does not mutate/create `plan` artifact (and any expected non-plan outputs still occur). Include negative control where diagnosis is absent to ensure gate condition is meaningful.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: multi-step orchestration error (cross-stage artifact propagation through the end-to-end chain), potentially compounded by agentic step behavior. Required setup: execute full pipeline in sequence and verify grounding generated upstream affects final implementation plan downstream. This is explicitly a full agent pipeline across stages, matching Workflow definition. Rejected lower levels because behavior text explicitly requires 'full multi-step agent run'.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end chain (`deep-dive -> evaluate/write-plan -> implement`) with controlled inputs where grounding should materially change downstream plan content. Assert final `plan` includes decisions traceable to produced `grounding`, and stage-to-stage artifact lineage is preserved through the run.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: fallback-path wiring/state-integrity bug (missing optional input causes bad writes or cross-artifact corruption). Required setup: run implement flow with no `grounding` present and inspect artifact store invariants before/after. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; correctness does not depend on model-quality variance; single-stage fallback, not multi-step workflow. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is about safe fallback and artifact integrity under missing data, which is reproducible with in-process modules.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize artifact store with unrelated artifacts, ensure `grounding` is absent, execute implement stage, then assert: `plan` is produced and schema-valid, unrelated artifacts are unchanged, and no accidental creation/overwrite of non-target artifact slots occurs.
